steps.title: Use the trial outcomes
steps.content: The student wants to present the study's results, so the sentence must state what the trials found. The relevant notes compare how often macaques called out to and gestured at two types of humans.

steps.title: Separate results from setup
steps.content: Background observations, the researchers' goal, and the study design explain why the study happened, but they are not the results. The result is the higher frequency of behavior toward humans holding food.

steps.title: Reject setup details
steps.content:
• A: reports that macaques called and gestured more at humans holding food than at empty-handed humans.
  • B: gives background and study setup.
  • C: states the research question.
  • D: describes what the researchers studied, not what they found.
The answer is A.
